questions.question: Explain the Storm cluster architecture including Nimbus, Supervisor, and ZooKeeper. How do they work together?
keyPoints: Nimbus (Master): Distributes code across cluster, assigns tasks to supervisors, monitors topology health, handles failures
Supervisor (Worker): Runs on each worker machine, manages worker processes (JVMs), executes assigned spout/bolt tasks, reports heartbeat to Nimbus
ZooKeeper: Provides coordination between Nimbus and Supervisors, stores cluster state and configuration, enables fault tolerance through distributed synchronization
Workflow: Nimbus submits topology → ZooKeeper coordinates → Supervisors start workers → Workers execute spouts/bolts

questions.question: Describe Storm topology with Spouts and Bolts. Explain stream groupings and how Storm guarantees message processing. Provide an IoT example of temperature monitoring.
keyPoints: Topology: DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) of spouts and bolts representing data flow and processing logic
Spouts: Entry points that read from external sources (Kafka, databases, sensors), emit tuples, can replay on failure for reliability
Bolts: Processing components that receive tuples, transform/filter/aggregate data, emit zero or more output tuples
Stream Groupings control tuple distribution: Shuffle (random for load balancing), Fields (group by key for per-sensor aggregation), All (broadcast), Global (single task)
Guaranteed Processing: Storm tracks tuple tree lineage, uses ack (success) and fail (replay) callbacks, configurable timeout (default 30s), provides at-least-once semantics

IoT Example: SensorSpout reads from Kafka → ParseBolt parses JSON → FilterBolt detects anomalies (temperature > 50°C) → AggregateBolt computes averages per sensor using Fields Grouping → AlertBolt generates instant notifications → DatabaseBolt stores results
Parallelism: Multiple workers (processes) → Executors (threads) → Tasks (instances) enable horizontal scaling
Real-time capability: Sub-second latency for instant anomaly detection and alerting in IoT monitoring
